extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won his second consecutive sprint stage after a chaotic finale where VAN LERBERGHE Bert's positioning issues forced CAVENDISH Mark to launch early, allowing DÉMARE to time his bike throw perfectly against EWAN Caleb.
tactical_patterns: ROSA Diego solo break allowed exactly 4 minutes reveals peloton calibration to avoid catching too early before sprint points.
KÄMNA Lennard contested intermediate sprint for bonus seconds despite being 39s down, signaling GC defense strategy beyond mountain stages.
VAN LERBERGHE Bert's leadout disruption forced CAVENDISH Mark to sprint 200m+ too early, exposing Quick-Step's fragility without MØRKØV.
DÉMARE Arnaud wins second stage by sitting on EWAN Caleb's wheel through chaos rather than relying on train positioning.

rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Won on bike throw after positioning behind EWAN despite losing organized leadout, showing tactical flexibility sprinters rarely display
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Launched too early when VAN LERBERGHE taken out, indicates dependence on perfect positioning rather than raw speed at age 37
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: Contesting intermediate sprint 39s down GC suggests Bora treating bonus seconds as viable defense strategy on flat stages
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Led out DÉMARE perfectly but lost bike throw, second straight near-miss exposes closing speed deficiency
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Third in intermediate sprint points against DÉMARE shows consistent top-5 positioning ability even when not winning
